data.title: Hang Lung Properties: Revenue declined 11% YoY, but profit and tenant sales in Mainland China retail grew
data.description:
Revenue fell 11% YoY, with stable operating profit and a 3% rise in underlying net profit. Mainland China retail showed resilience with flat rental revenue and 4% tenant sales growth, while Hong Kong leasing softened. Strategic expansions and strong ESG progress underpin future growth.

data.title: Adidas Expected to Gain After Results, Buyback News — Market Talk
data.description: Adidas shares are seen gaining at the open after the German sportswear maker reported stronger-than-expected brand sales for the fourth quarter and announced a 1 billion-euro share buyback, Citi analysts say in a note. Share gains would come in the context of recent muted market performance, they say. Adidas closed at 143.50 euros. Shares have fallen 15% in the year so far and 45% over the past 12 months. (sarah.sloat@wsj.com)

data.title: Thailand Current Account Surplus Widens in December
data.description:
Thailand’s current account surplus widened to USD 3.10 billion in December 2025 from USD 2.62 billion a year earlier, supported by broad-based improvements across major components.

The trade surplus widened to USD 2.68 billion from USD 2.26 billion, as exports rose to USD 28.82 billion from USD 24.41 billion, while imports also increased to USD 26.15 billion from USD 22.15 billion.

Net services, primary income, and secondary income improved to USD 0.42 billion from USD 0.36 billion.

In addition, the financial account recorded a larger overall balance of USD 3.69 billion from USD 1.84 billion.

data.title: Thailand Retail Sales Growth Eases in November
data.description:
Retail sales in Thailand rose by 15.5% year-on-year in November 2025, easing from an upwardly revised 66.8% climb in the previous month.

This marked the third consecutive month of growth, albeit at a softer pace, as sales growth moderated in other retail stores (37.4% vs 134.4% in October) and non-durable goods (4.6% vs 5.6%), particularly in pharmaceutical and medical goods.

At the same time, trade declined in durable goods (-1.5% vs 0.5%) and motor vehicles and automotive fuel (- 6.9% vs -1.5%).

Meanwhile, activity increased in department stores, supermarkets, and general stores (5.1% vs 2%).

On a monthly basis, retail sales dropped sharply by 35% in November, following an upwardly revised 27.1% growth in the preceding period.

data.title: Kimberly-Clark, Suzano Joint Venture Could Face U.K. Competition Probe
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

The U.K.'s competition regulator said it was considering whether to launch an investigation into a proposed joint venture between Kleenex maker Kimberly-Clark and pulp manufacturer Suzano.

Kimberly-Clark, which makes Scott toilet paper and Huggies diapers, said in June that it struck a $3.4 billion deal to sell most of its international tissue business to Brazil's Suzano, creating a joint venture in which it would hold a 49% interest. The companies said at the time Suzano would also have the option to purchase Kimberly-Clark's remaining ownership interest in the future.

The U.K.'s Competition and Markets Authority on Friday said that it was inviting comments from interested parties before deciding whether to investigate if the deal would harm competition in the U.K.

The CMA said the invitation to comment was the first part of its information-gathering process and that no formal investigation into the transaction had yet been launched.

Parties have until Feb. 20 to put forward their comments, it said.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: The Kansai Electric Power Company, Incorporated Nine Months Income Drops
data.description:
TOKYO (dpa-AFX) - The Kansai Electric Power Company, Incorporated (KAEPF) released a profit for nine months that Dropped, from the same period last year

The company's earnings totaled JPY340.199 billion, or JPY305.37 per share. This compares with JPY362.294 billion, or JPY396.43 per share, last year.

The company's revenue for the period fell 6.4% to JPY2.949 trillion from JPY3.152 trillion last year.

The Kansai Electric Power Company, Incorporated earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: JPY340.199 Bln. vs. JPY362.294 Bln. last year.

-EPS: JPY305.37 vs. JPY396.43 last year.

-Revenue: JPY2.949 Tn vs. JPY3.152 Tn last year.

-Guidance:

Full year EPS guidance: JPY 323.14

Full year revenue guidance: JPY 4.050 T

data.title: Canadian Dollar Rises Against Majors
data.description:
CANBERA (dpa-AFX) - The Canadian dollar strengthened against other major currencies in the Asian session on Friday.

The Canadian dollar rose to a 1-week high of 113.99 against the yen, a 4-day high of 0.9446 against the Australian dollar and nearly a 2-week high of 1.6109 against the euro, from yesterday's closing quotes of 113.51, 0.9509 and 1.6148, respectively.

Against the U.S. dollar, the loonie edged up to 1.3510 from an early low of 1.3546.

If the loonie extends its uptrend, it is likely to find resistance around 115.00 against the yen, 0.93 against the aussie, 1.60 against the euro and 1.33 against the greenback.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: France's Economy Slowed Pace in 2025, Hit by Tarrifs — Update
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

France's economy last year grew at its weakest pace since 2020, hampered by the effect of U.S. tariffs that buffeted European economies.

Gross domestic product in the eurozone's second-largest economy rose 0.9% in 2025, down from 1.1% in 2024 and 1.6% in 2023, France's statistics agency Insee said Friday.

After two years of contributing positively to GDP, foreign trade negatively hit growth in 2025, Insee said. Exports slowed sharply, rising 1.4% in 2025 compared with 2.4% in 2024, as the ramp-up in U.S. tariffs on imports of European Union goods hurt France's exporters. Imports into France jumped 2.9%, after falling 1.3% in 2024.

Meanwhile, household spending also slowed, though government expenditure picked up, the data showed.

The International Monetary Fund said earlier in January that it expects French GDP to pick up to 1.0% in 2026, slower than the eurozone average.

However, while confidence indexes among firms and households have picked up a little lately, underlying dynamics are still weak, Fabien Bossy, economist at Paris-based Societe Generale, said in a note to clients.

Consumer spending dropped in December, Insee said separately Friday.

In the fourth quarter, growth declined to 0.2%, down from 0.5% in the three months through September, matching consensus expectations from economists polled by The Wall Street Journal.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: German 2-year yields set for weekly drop ahead of inflation data
data.description:
By Stefano Rebaudo

German two-year government bond yields were set for their biggest weekly drop since October, with investors betting the European Central Bank will factor in the deflationary drag from a stronger euro as it shapes its policy outlook.

Investors are also awaiting the release of January inflation data from German states later on Friday.

The euro hit a five-year high against the greenback on Tuesday after U.S. President Donald Trump said the value of the dollar was "great" when asked if he thought it had declined too much.

German 2-year yields (DE2YT=RR), which are more sensitive to expectations for policy rates, were up 0.5 basis points (bps) at 2.06%. They were on track for a weekly decline of 6.5 bps, the biggest since October.

Money markets priced in a chance of around 30% for an ECB rate cut in September (EURESTECBM6X7=ICAP), up from less than 10% a week ago, while indicating a 20% probability of a rate hike in April 2027, down from 50%. (EURESTECBM11X12=ICAP)

Germany’s 10-year government bond yield (DE10YT=RR), the euro area’s benchmark, rose 1.5 bps to 2.87%.

Long-dated U.S. Treasuries were sold in early London trading on Friday, with benchmark 10-year yields up 4 bps at 4.27%, on speculation Trump would nominate former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh to head the U.S. central bank and that he would push to reduce the amount of bonds the bank owns.

The 30-year yield (DE30YT=RR) was up 1.5 bps at 3.49%. It rose to 3.556% in late December, its highest since summer 2011.

The yield gap between French government bonds and safe-haven Bunds (DE10FR10=RR) - a market gauge of the risk premium investors demand to hold French debt - widened to 58 bps after hitting a fresh 19-month low of 55.26 bps on Tuesday.

Italy’s 10-year government bond yields rose 0.5 bps to 3.52%. The gap versus Bunds was at 58 bps after tightening to 53.50 bps in mid-January, its lowest level since August 2008.

data.title: CBOT soybeans to slide to $10.58-1/4
data.description:
Key points:
  • For more technical analyses, please clickTECH/C

The CBOT soybean March contract (SH26) is expected to slide to $10.58-1/4 per bushel, as it is about to break the support at $10.66-1/2 and a rising trendline.

The uptrend from $10.37-3/4 may have reversed, as suggested by the deep fall on Thursday. The Jan. 26 low of $10.58-1/2 serves as a target.

Resistance is at $10.71, a break above which may lead to a gain into $10.73-1/4 to $10.75-3/4 range.

On the daily chart, a shooting star formed on Thursday, which is likely to be confirmed by a black candle on Friday. Together, they suggest a reversal of the short uptrend from $10.37-3/4.

A retracement analysis on the downtrend from $11.74 reveals a target zone of $10.47-1/4 to $10.57-1/2.

* Wang Tao is a Reuters market analyst for commodities and energy technicals. Views expressed are his own.

** No information in this analysis should be considered as being business, financial, or legal advice. Each reader should consult their own professional or other advisers for business, financial, or legal advice regarding the products mentioned in the analyses.

data.title: Adidas jumps on solid Q4 sales, share buyback launch
data.description:
** Shares in Adidas jump 6.3% in early Frankfurt trade after the German sportswear maker posted solid Q4 sales, announced share buyback program late on Thursday

** The company's Q4 sales ex-Yeezy were up 11%, ahead of 9% consensus, Deutsche Bank says

** Adidas also said it would launch a share buyback of up to 1 billion euros ($1.19 billion), starting in early February

** "It will be well received by investors given the current share price, earnings accretion and increased confidence in the 2026 outlook," DB adds

** The stock is seen at the top of the German blue-chip index

** The company will release full results along with its annual outlook in early March

($1 = 0.8385 euros)

data.title: Norway Jobseekers Lowest in 9 Months
data.description:
The number of people registered as out of work in Norway fell for a second month in a row to a seasonally adjusted 62.6 thousand in January 2025 from a downwardly revised 63.6 thousand in the previous month.

This marks the lowest since April 2025, corresponding to 2.1% of the workforce.

Unemployment declined for almost half of the occupational groups, with the lowest rate in academic professions, as well as health, nursing and care (0.9% of the workforce) and the highest among construction (3.6%).

Regionally, Troms and Nordland recorded the lowest jobless rates, at 1.3% and 1.8%, respectively, while Østfold was the highest at 3.1%.

Meanwhile, 56,900 vacancies were advertised in January, equivalent to 1,900 per working day, which is 3% lower than a year ago.

On a non-seasonally adjusted basis, the number of fully unemployed people rose to 68.9 thousand in January from 62.4 thousand in December 2025.
